query: 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary status: Byron Donalds candidacy, opponents, Trump endorsement, polling, competitors dropping out. Who is running against Donalds and what are the polls showing as of mid-2026?
answer:
I've completed the research. Here's a summary of the key findings on the 2026 Florida Republican gubernatorial primary status as of early-mid July 2026:

**Byron Donalds** is the dominant frontrunner. He announced in February 2025 already holding Trump's endorsement (which Trump has repeatedly reaffirmed), has raised ~$65–67M (including a record $22M in Q1 2026), and leads all polls by roughly 40+ points.

**Polling:** Emerson (April) had him at 46%; Fabrizio/Lee at 54%; Associated Industries of Florida (June) at 54%; Change Research (May) showed a 39-point lead. Opponents are all stuck in single digits.

**Opponents still running (field NOT cleared):** Lt. Gov. Jay Collins, businessman James Fishback, former House Speaker Paul Renner, and Surfside mayor Charles Burkett. **Casey DeSantis did not enter** — she appears only in hypothetical polls, where Donalds still wins ~44–7.

**No one has dropped out.** Instead, the drama is legal: Collins is suing to remove Fishback from the ballot, with a two-day hearing set to begin **July 21, 2026**. Donalds has skipped debates, calling his rivals "not viable."

On the Democratic side, David Jolly leads his primary; one May poll even showed Jolly ahead of Donalds in a hypothetical general, though Florida is rated Solid Republican. Full synthesis, sources, and structured data points have been recorded.

key_facts: Florida 2026 gubernatorial primary is August 18, 2026; general election November 3, 2026. Gov. Ron DeSantis is term-limited.
U.S. Rep. Byron Donalds (FL-19, Naples) is the clear GOP frontrunner and announced his candidacy in February 2025 with Trump's endorsement already in hand.
President Trump first backed Donalds in February 2025 and has repeatedly reaffirmed the endorsement (including a Truth Social post calling him a 'TOTAL WINNER').
Donalds has raised approximately $65-67 million, including a record $22 million in Q1 2026 — far more than any rival.
The field has NOT cleared: opponents still running are Lt. Gov. Jay Collins, businessman James Fishback, former House Speaker Paul Renner, and Surfside mayor Charles Burkett.
All opponents poll in single digits; Donalds leads by roughly 40+ points across multiple 2026 polls.
Emerson College (April 2026): Donalds 46%, Collins 4%, Fishback 4%, 39% undecided; in a test with Casey DeSantis, Donalds 44% to DeSantis 7%.
Fabrizio, Lee & Associates (2026): Donalds 54% (+9 since January), Fishback 9%, Collins 7%, Renner 2%.
Associated Industries of Florida / Center for Political Strategy (June 2026): Donalds 54%.
Change Research (May 13-16, 2026): Donalds leads GOP primary by 39 points.
Casey DeSantis has NOT entered the race — she appears only in hypothetical polling matchups, which Donalds still wins decisively.
No major candidate has dropped out; instead Lt. Gov. Jay Collins is suing to remove James Fishback from the ballot, with a two-day hearing set to begin July 21, 2026.
Donalds has skipped candidate debates (RPOF 'Sunshine State Showdown' and an early-July online debate), calling his opponents 'not viable.'

Democratic frontrunner David Jolly leads his primary by ~15 points; one May 2026 Change Research poll showed Jolly ahead of Donalds in a hypothetical general election, though Florida is rated Solid Republican.
DeSantis job approval was 50% in the April 2026 Emerson poll; Gov. DeSantis has not endorsed Donalds and called the debate dynamics a 'farce.'
